question_text: The President, along with the Council of Ministers, ............. to visit India next week. Select the most appropriate option to complete the above sentence.
solution:
The subject of the sentence is ”The President”, which is singular. The phrase ”along with the
Council of Ministers” is a modifying phrase and does not affect the subject-verb agreement.
Therefore, the verb should also be singular.
”wishes” is the singular form of the verb and agrees with the singular subject ”The
President”.
”wish” is plural and would be incorrect here.
”will wish” changes the tense unnecessarily.
”is wishing” is awkward and not appropriate in this context.
Hence, the correct form is: ”The President, along with the Council of Ministers, wishes to
visit India next week.”
Quick Tip
Ignore interrupting phrases like ”along with”, ”as well as”, etc., when determining
subject-verb agreement.
